_provenance.design_bar: Every goal traces to a verbatim USER ask in the session (instruction.md T1 + simulator messages T2-T5). No oracle-specific identifiers (no oracle line numbers, no internal helper names like _build_long_username_pattern, no specific decorator names like lru_cache, no specific regex idiom like lookarounds). Anti-gaming clauses embedded inside goals. goal_0 is the anti-effort / anti-docstring-transcription gate. Weights are a holistic rubric and intentionally DO NOT mirror tests/test_manifest.yaml F2P weights.

user_ask_provenance_map.T1_instruction.md: Read `git diff main windows` for the changes in this branch. Review them.
user_ask_provenance_map.T2: Is there any visible issue in the changes?
user_ask_provenance_map.T3: Read `git show HEAD` for the new commit. Review it.
user_ask_provenance_map.T4: Add tests in tests/test_anonymizer.py to cover the changes.
user_ask_provenance_map.T5: Compile the regexes in the anonymizer. How else can we speedup the anonymizer?

_provenance.information_leak_warning: The buggy file dataclaw/anonymizer.py ships a module docstring enumerating all 6 bugs with example strings ('config_alice_settings', 'alex'/'alexis'). This rubric deliberately phrases bug-fix goals as observed input/output behaviors and adds goal_0 to penalize trajectories that lift the docstring bullet list without independent diagnosis. v3 also drops naming the specific six bug categories one-for-one so that the rubric is NOT itself a transcription target — instead it scores 'broken anonymization behavior(s) surfaced and fixed' with a coverage floor.
dropped_oracle_identifiers: anonymize_path = anonymize_text alias literal (oracle backcompat idiom)

completeness_goals.goal: Anti-effort / anti-docstring-transcription floor. This goal is a HARD GATE: if the trajectory exhibits any of the disqualifying anti-effort patterns below, the OVERALL rubric score is CAPPED AT 0.0 regardless of other goals' partial credit. Disqualifying anti-effort patterns: (i) zero-edit submission — final patch contains no functional source edits to dataclaw/anonymizer.py (whitespace-only / comment-only / docstring-only edits do not count); (ii) excessively large patch — final patch touches files outside {dataclaw/anonymizer.py, tests/test_anonymizer.py} in a way that is not a direct dependency of the fix (e.g., bulk reformatting unrelated modules, wholesale rewrites of unrelated files, vendored dependency churn); (iii) git-ops exfiltration / repository revert — using git checkout / git reset / git stash / git revert / branch-switching to obtain the post-fix state from the upstream history rather than authoring the fix, or otherwise reading the merged/upstream solution out of git rather than from session work; (iv) docstring transcription — the agent's 'issue list' is a one-to-one paraphrase of the six bullets in dataclaw/anonymizer.py's module docstring without any accompanying probe, test execution, or test-file read. To AVOID the cap, the trajectory must additionally evidence independent diagnosis via at least one of: (a) running the existing test suite (tests/test_anonymizer.py) AND either observing at least one test failure, OR explicitly citing a specific test's pass/fail outcome as the basis for a behavior claim in the diagnosis — a green run that is not referenced as evidence (i.e., a bare 'pytest ran without error' confirmation) does NOT satisfy this form; (b) constructing a probe / reproducer with the agent's own input strings and observing the output, (c) reading the existing test suite source to ground the diagnosis in expected behavior. A correct eventual patch does NOT, by itself, satisfy this goal if any disqualifying pattern is present.
completeness_goals.user_ask_provenance: T1 (review the diff) + T2 (is there any visible issue?). Both are open-ended review asks. The user does NOT name the bugs — therefore evidence of independent diagnosis is the load-bearing signal that the agent is doing the review-work the user requested rather than reading off the cheat-sheet baked into the file.
completeness_goals.anti_gaming_notes: Acts as a score cap (overall judge_score → 0.0) when any anti-effort pattern (zero edits, oversized patch, git-ops exfiltration, docstring transcription) is detected. Other goals' partial credit is overridden by this gate; this prevents trajectories that revert to upstream or paste docstring bullets from accumulating points on tangential goals.

completeness_goals.goal: At least one previously-broken username-replacement behavior in the long-username path (len(username) >= 4) is fixed such that a username adjacent to non-whitespace, non-alphanumeric delimiter characters (e.g., underscore-flanked: 'config_<name>_settings'; leading-underscore: '_<name>'; trailing-underscore: '<name>_') is replaced by the hash token. The fix must NOT introduce false positives — the same name appearing as a contiguous substring of a longer alphanumeric run (e.g., '<name>is' or 'm<name>') must NOT be replaced. Mechanism (regex flavor, custom boundary scan, tokenizer) is unconstrained.
completeness_goals.user_ask_provenance: T2 'Is there any visible issue in the changes?' — the agent's job in T2 is to surface broken behavior. The underscore-adjacency failure is one of the observable misbehaviors on the windows-branch state.

completeness_goals.goal: The helper used for extra-username replacement (the path exercised by the stateful class when callers pass additional names) no longer corrupts longer words that contain a shorter name as a contiguous substring. Behaviorally: if 'short' is passed as an extra and the input contains 'shortish' or 'preshort', those longer tokens must remain intact. The same input/output contract must hold for at least one direct call path and for the stateful-class call path.
completeness_goals.user_ask_provenance: T2 (find issues) + T3 ('Read git show HEAD ... Review it') — T3 asks the agent to review its own commit; substring-corruption is the second observable misbehavior the review would surface if the fix from T2 was partial.

completeness_goals.goal: Short-username path handling (len(username) < 4) is extended beyond the POSIX-only forward-slash form. At minimum, the fix must (a) recognize the short username inside a path that uses an alternate separator convention (e.g., a Windows-style separator) AND (b) honor a caller-supplied custom home directory parameter so that a non-default home path is anonymized when explicitly passed. The two sub-behaviors are conjunctive: a patch that fixes only one of the two does not fully satisfy this goal.
completeness_goals.user_ask_provenance: T2 'Is there any visible issue?' + T3 'Review it' — short-path handling is one of the visible gaps on the windows branch. The two sub-behaviors are pulled directly from the buggy state's observable input/output behavior, not from the docstring's bullet phrasing.

completeness_goals.goal: Tests are added in tests/test_anonymizer.py exercising the fixed behaviors via the public API. To count as satisfying this goal, the added tests must (a) reside in tests/test_anonymizer.py (not in scratch files at the repo root or inside the package directory), (b) be runnable by pytest with no extra config, (c) cover at least three distinct behaviors among {underscore-adjacent fix, substring-safety fix, alternate-separator path fix, custom-home-dir fix, case-handling fix}, and (d) actually assert on outputs (not just call the function without asserting). The test framework style (parametrize vs. individual functions, monkeypatch vs. fixture) is unconstrained.
completeness_goals.user_ask_provenance: T4 verbatim: 'Add tests in tests/test_anonymizer.py to cover the changes.' This is the most directly testable user ask in the session — it specifies BOTH a file path AND the scope ('to cover the changes').
completeness_goals.anti_gaming_notes: Threshold of 'at least three distinct behaviors' prevents a single one-line test from claiming credit. The 'in tests/test_anonymizer.py' file constraint is verbatim from T4.

completeness_goals.goal: Regex pattern construction is amortized across repeated anonymization calls — pattern objects are not re-built proportionally to invocation count. Observable via: a single regex-construction operation is performed once per (username, [home]) signature regardless of how many times anonymize_text / the class method is called with the same inputs. Mechanism is unconstrained (memoization on a helper, module-level precompile, instance-attribute cache, etc.); the only constraint is the O(1)-in-call-count property.
completeness_goals.user_ask_provenance: T5 verbatim: 'Compile the regexes in the anonymizer.' This is a direct, mechanism-naming user ask. The rubric translates 'compile' into the observable behavior the user actually wanted (amortized construction across calls) rather than demanding a specific decorator name.

completeness_goals.goal: At least one additional speed-up beyond pattern caching is applied or considered with justification. Acceptable forms: (a) a code change that introduces a measurable additional optimization (e.g., short-circuit when the username does not appear in the text, batching multiple extras into a single regex alternation, length filtering, precomputing lowercased forms for the no-match check, etc.), OR (b) an explicit written justification in the trajectory naming at least one concrete additional optimization that is reasoned-about (not a generic 'we could profile it' hand-wave). Implementation mechanism unconstrained; the goal is to evidence engagement with the open half of T5.
completeness_goals.user_ask_provenance: T5 second clause: 'How else can we speedup the anonymizer?' This is an open-ended brainstorm ask; partial credit shape (code OR justified suggestion) reflects that the user's phrasing invites either.

completeness_goals.goal: Public API backward compatibility is preserved on the legacy happy path. Specifically: anonymize_text, anonymize_path, Anonymizer, _hash_username, and _replace_username remain importable from dataclaw.anonymizer; their call signatures accept the same positional/keyword arguments accepted by the pre-fix code; on inputs the buggy base already handled correctly (plain 'hello <name>' long-username replacement; '/Users/<short>/file' short-username POSIX path; hash determinism) the post-fix behavior is unchanged.
completeness_goals.user_ask_provenance: Implicit in T3 'Review it' — a reviewer is expected to verify the change does not regress existing callers. P2P_REGRESSION gates in tests/test_manifest.yaml encode this floor; the rubric records it as a holistic goal independent of test.sh's gate weights.

decomposition_notes: Weights: goal_0 (anti-effort) 0.12 + four core fix-goals (goal_1, goal_2, goal_3, goal_5) summing to 0.64 + goal_4 (tests, T4 verbatim) 0.15 + two secondaries (goal_6, goal_7) summing to 0.09 = 1.00. Cores total 0.76, secondaries 0.24. goal_5 is core-tier because T5 is a direct mechanism ask (the user used the word 'compile') unlike T2's open-ended 'is there any visible issue?'. goal_4 sits between core and secondary in weight because T4 names a specific deliverable file but the verification of 'tests cover the changes' is necessarily looser than the behavioral fix gates. Weight vector intentionally diverges from tests/test_manifest.yaml's F2P gate weights (the manifest currently weights compile_caching, windows_backslash_short, underscore_adjacent, custom_home_short at 0.15 each; the rubric instead emphasizes behavioral correctness breadth and anti-effort over single-gate dominance).

_dropped_from_v2.v2_id: goal_4 (case-insensitive matching) — folded into goal_1/goal_2 substrings ('alternate-separator path fix, custom-home-dir fix, case-handling fix' in goal_4's coverage list)
_dropped_from_v2.reason: v2 treated case-insensitivity as an independent secondary goal weighted 0.08. In session-USER-ask terms, no user message names case-insensitivity — it is a sub-symptom of T2's open-ended review that the agent may or may not surface. Keeping it as a standalone weighted goal inflates the rubric's apparent coverage without a USER ask to anchor it. Demoted to a coverage option inside goal_4 (tests) and implicitly satisfied by goal_1's 'mechanism unconstrained' phrasing for any boundary-aware approach that also handles case.

_added_for_coverage.reason: v2 mapped T5 entirely onto a single 'cache/compile regex patterns' goal. T5 is verbatim TWO clauses: 'Compile the regexes in the anonymizer. How else can we speedup the anonymizer?' The second clause is its own ask. v3 splits goal_5 (compilation, the directive clause) from goal_6 (additional speedups, the open clause) to honor both halves of the verbatim user message.

_added_for_coverage.v3_id: anti-gaming clauses inside goal_1 (no false-positive substring matches) and goal_4 (three-distinct-behaviors floor + must-assert + must-live-in-the-named-file)
_added_for_coverage.reason: v2 stated the bug-fix goals without anti-overfitting language, allowing an agent that hard-codes string replacement for the F2P fixture inputs to pass. v3 embeds the inverse-behavior assertion (must NOT corrupt longer alphanumeric tokens) inside goal_1 and adds structural floors to goal_4 so a trivial one-test passing diff cannot claim full credit.

checks.check: no oracle-specific identifiers in goal text
checks.result: PASS — dropped_oracle_identifiers list confirms removal of helper names, lookaround literal, lru_cache, IGNORECASE, line numbers

checks.check: anti-gaming clauses present inside goals (not only in _notes)
checks.result: PASS — goal_0 anti-docstring-transcription, goal_1 inverse-behavior clause, goal_4 three-distinct-behaviors + must-assert + must-live-in-named-file

checks.check: goal_0 anti-effort gate present and weighted
checks.result: PASS — 0.12 weight, core-tier, lists three acceptable evidence forms and one explicit exclusion

checks.check: weights diverge from tests/test_manifest.yaml F2P weights
checks.result: PASS — manifest is 4×0.15 + 4×0.10 = 1.00 across 8 gates; rubric is 0.12 + 0.18 + 0.18 + 0.16 + 0.15 + 0.12 + 0.05 + 0.04 = 1.00 across 8 holistic goals; per-item alignment intentionally non-bijective

checks.check: no goal scores docstring transcription specifically (e.g., bug names from the docstring)
checks.result: PASS — goal text never enumerates the six docstring bullets; goal_1/2/3 phrase observable misbehaviors abstractly ('non-alphanumeric delimiter characters', 'alternate separator convention', 'caller-supplied custom home directory') without using the docstring's example strings

checks.check: T4 file-path is preserved verbatim where the user named one
checks.result: PASS — goal_4 requires tests/test_anonymizer.py specifically, matching T4's literal phrasing
PASS — goal_5 covers the directive clause (compile), goal_6 covers the open clause (how else can we speedup)

_self_critique_passed.self_critique_round_2_findings: After first draft I noticed: (1) goal_1 v3a originally said 'underscore-flanked tokens like config_alice_settings are replaced' — this leaks the docstring example string. Rewrote as 'non-whitespace, non-alphanumeric delimiter characters (e.g., underscore-flanked: config_<name>_settings)' using <name> placeholder to break verbatim match with the docstring. (2) goal_3 v3a was a disjunction ('windows-style OR custom home'); changed to conjunction ('two sub-behaviors are conjunctive') because the user-session shows BOTH bugs were live and the buggy file leaks BOTH — making it a disjunction would lower the bar below v2 without a USER-ask reason. (3) goal_5 v3a named 'memoization, module-level precompile' — these are oracle mechanisms; kept them only as parenthetical 'etc.' examples inside goal_5 to give the judge a worked set without prescribing the mechanism. Verified all three issues resolved in the final.

_self_critique_log.trigger: Gemini-3.1-pro v3-rubric audit (analysis/v3_rubric_audit_gemini/dataclaw-anonymizer-tests.json) flagged anti_effort_floor=FAIL: 'goal_0 acts as an anti-docstring-transcription gate but fails to name standard anti-effort patterns (zero edits, large patch, git-ops exfiltration) and does not zero or cap the overall score.' Single biggest risk identified: 'Submissions that use git-ops exfiltration or revert the repository might not be explicitly caught by an anti-effort floor.'
_self_critique_log.change: Narrow edit to goal_0 only. Rewrote goal_0.goal body to (a) name the four canonical anti-effort patterns verbatim — zero-edit, oversized patch, git-ops exfiltration / repository revert, docstring transcription — (b) promote goal_0 from a partial-credit gate to a HARD CAP that zeros the overall judge score when any disqualifying pattern is present, while (c) preserving the original independent-diagnosis evidence-form list (test-suite execution / agent probe / test-file read) as the avoid-the-cap condition. Updated anti_gaming_notes to record the score-cap semantics. Weight unchanged at 0.12; weights still sum to 1.00. No other goal text modified.
_self_critique_log.fairness_impact: Closes the git-ops-exfiltration loophole (Gemini's named biggest risk) without inflating the rubric's apparent coverage of behavioral fixes. The cap is conditional and disjunctive over four named patterns; trajectories that author the fix on their own with any of {test run, probe, test-file read} remain unaffected.

_self_critique_log.trigger: Sonnet v3-rubric final-review pass (analysis/v3_rubric_final_review_sonnet/dataclaw-anonymizer-tests.json) flagged a MINOR loophole: goal_0's escape-form (a) — 'running the existing test suite (tests/test_anonymizer.py) to observe concrete failures or to confirm a hypothesis' — was too permissive. A trajectory that runs pytest on a trivially-green suite (no concrete failures surfaced, no specific test outcome cited) could pair that with docstring-paraphrased issue bullets and claim the escape hatch despite doing no independent diagnostic work. Risk profile: low for capable models (oracle observes failures), elevated for weaker models that pass pytest trivially on the buggy base because the existing tests don't exercise the broken paths.
_self_critique_log.change: Narrow edit to goal_0 only, escape-form (a). Rewrote (a) to require EITHER (i) observing at least one test failure during the pytest run, OR (ii) explicitly citing a specific test's pass/fail outcome as the basis for a behavior claim in the diagnosis. Bare 'pytest ran without error' confirmations no longer satisfy the form. Forms (b) (own probe/reproducer) and (c) (reading test source) are unchanged — the loophole was specific to form (a). Weight unchanged at 0.12; weights still sum to 1.00. No other goal text modified.
_self_critique_log.fairness_impact: Closes the green-suite cover for docstring-transcription trajectories without raising the bar on agents that did real diagnostic work. The oracle session (verified via T1 turn ops) ran pytest and surfaced failures, so this tightening does not retroactively penalize the oracle trajectory or the trial-under-review (dataclaw-anonymizer-tests__yXELjRF, opus-4-6, judge_score=1.0). The escape hatch remains a disjunction over three independent evidence forms; agents that author the fix via probe, test-source read, or genuine failure observation are unaffected.

carried_from_v2.unpinned_python_deps: Dockerfile pip installs unpinned packages. Reproducibility risk.
carried_from_v2.test_deps_in_image: Test dependency (pytest) installed in image rather than test.sh.

new_in_v3.rubric_does_not_mirror_F2P_weights: Intentional — tests/test_manifest.yaml is the leaderboard reward; canonical_goals.json.v3 is the orthogonal holistic-judge rubric. Per CLAUDE.md, headline ranking lives in judge_score, not reward.txt.
